name: Chakrasamvara
shortDescription: Chakrasamvara is a wrathful meditational deity (ishtadevata) of the Anuttarayoga Tantra class in Vajrayana Buddhism, often depicted in yab-yum with his consort Vajravarahi. He represents the union of skillful means and wisdom, leading to enlightenment.
description: Chakrasamvara is a prominent deity in Vajrayana Buddhism, particularly revered in Tibetan Buddhism. He is a wrathful manifestation embodying the union of compassion and wisdom, crucial for achieving enlightenment. Often depicted in yab-yum with his consort Vajravarahi, their embrace symbolizes the indivisibility of skillful means (upaya) and wisdom (prajna). Chakrasamvara's iconography is complex, typically featuring multiple arms and heads, each holding various ritual implements that represent different aspects of Buddhist practice and philosophy. His dark blue or black skin signifies his fierce nature and the overcoming of negative emotions. He is associated with the Heruka deities and is considered a manifestation of ultimate reality. The Chakrasamvara Tantra, a key text associated with him, outlines the practices and visualizations necessary for realizing his enlightened state. The deity is frequently invoked in tantric rituals aimed at transforming negative emotions into wisdom and achieving spiritual liberation. His worship is central to various lineages within Tibetan Buddhism, where he is seen as a powerful protector and guide on the path to enlightenment. Some traditions link him to Shiva, seeing him as a Buddhist adaptation of Shiva's powerful and transformative aspects. The worship of Chakrasamvara involves intricate mandalas, mantras, and visualizations, all designed to facilitate the practitioner's identification with the deity and the realization of their own Buddha-nature. He is a central figure in the practice of Anuttarayoga Tantra, the highest class of tantric teachings.

sources.title: The Chakrasamvara Tantra: The Discourse of Sri Heruka
sources.author: Kajiya, Ronima
